definitions.text: Boundary, land within a boundary. A boundary; a border or frontier. .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller} A boundary-post or fence. A stone or post used to indicate position and guide travellers. The line indicating an athlete's starting-point. A type of small region or principality. A common, or area of common land, especially among early Germanic peoples.
A boundary; a border or frontier. .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
To put a mark on (something); to make (something) recognizable by a mark; to label or write on (something).
examples: to mark a box or bale of merchandise
to mark clothing with one's name

definitions.text: To leave a mark (often an undesirable or unwanted one) on (something).
examples: See where this pencil has marked the paper.
The floor was marked with wine and blood.

definitions.text: To have a long-lasting negative impact on (someone or something).
A half pound, a traditional unit of mass equivalent to 226.8 g.
Similar half-pound units in other measurement systems, chiefly used for gold and silver.
A half pound, a former English and Scottish currency equivalent to 13 shillings and fourpence and notionally equivalent to a mark of sterling silver.
A former currency of Germany and West Germany.
